info.name: 04 Notifications and Auth
info.description:
Module owner: Kesavanabinayan12@gmail.com

Scope: signup, login, notifications, and role or auth-related scaffolding.

HTTP methods scaffolded: GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE.

Important: real bearer tokens do not exist yet in this repo because the backend auth flow has not been implemented. Keep `{{accessToken}}` and `{{adminToken}}` empty until the API starts returning them.

request.description:
Intended endpoint: `POST /auth/signup`
Expected role: public
Expected status: `201 Created`
Current state: placeholder only until backend auth exists.
TODO: Replace the payload with the final signup DTO and validation rules.

exec: console.log('Login response is not JSON yet.');
console.log('No token or accessToken field found in response.');

request.description:
Intended endpoint: `POST /auth/login`
Expected role: public
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder only until backend auth exists.
Behavior when implemented: saves `token` or `accessToken` from the response into `{{accessToken}}`.

exec: console.log('Admin login response is not JSON yet.');
console.log('No token or accessToken field found in response.');

request.description:
Intended endpoint: `POST /auth/login`
Expected role: public login flow that returns an admin bearer token for an admin account.
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder only until backend auth exists.
Behavior when implemented: saves `token` or `accessToken` from the response into `{{adminToken}}`.

Intended endpoint: `GET /notifications?read=false`
Expected role: authenticated user
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder until the backend both implements auth and issues real bearer tokens.
TODO: Replace the query parameters if unread filtering is modelled differently.

Intended endpoint: `PATCH /notifications/{{notificationId}}`
Expected role: notification owner
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder until the backend both implements auth and issues real bearer tokens.
TODO: Update this to the final mark-as-read route if the API uses a subresource.

Intended endpoint: `DELETE /notifications/{{notificationId}}`
Expected role: notification owner
Expected status: `204 No Content`
Current state: placeholder until the backend both implements auth and issues real bearer tokens.
TODO: Keep this only if notifications are actually deletable in the final design.

Intended endpoint: `POST /auth/login`
Expected role: public
Expected status: `401 Unauthorized`
Current state: placeholder until the backend auth flow exists.
TODO: Use this request as the reusable auth validation failure example.

Intended endpoint: `PATCH /users/roles`
Expected role: `ADMIN`
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder until the backend auth and role-management flow exists.
TODO: Replace the payload and path with the final role-management contract if the project exposes one.

Intended endpoint: `PATCH /users/roles`
Expected role: `ADMIN`
Expected status: `403 Forbidden` when run with `{{accessToken}}`
Current state: placeholder until the backend auth and role-management flow exists.
TODO: Keep this request as the role-management access-control proof.

Intended endpoint: `GET /auth/oauth2/status`
Expected role: public or callback flow
Expected status: `200 OK`
Current state: placeholder until OAuth or JWT auth is actually implemented.
TODO: Replace this with the final OAuth status or callback verification endpoint when it exists.
